proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_012 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65; behaves like patient age) is positively associated with longer pfs_months in the full cohort.
Higher feature_005 (ordinal 0/1/2) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the full cohort.
Patients with feature_006=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_006=0.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: pfs_months increases by 0.176 months per 1-unit increase in feature_012 (Pearson r=0.70, p<1e-300).
Univariate OLS: each 1-unit increase in feature_005 reduces pfs_months by 1.18 months (p<1e-300).
Mean pfs_months 3.62 (feature_006=1, n=14969) vs 5.15 (feature_006=0, n=35031); difference -1.54 months (Welch p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001=1 (~10% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_028=1 (~35% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_018=1 (~18% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs 3.75 (f001=1) vs 4.80 (f001=0); diff -1.05 months (Welch p~1.5e-178).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013=1 (~35% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months and is the leading candidate treatment indicator (large signed effect, prevalence in the typical treatment range, no tight collinearity with other features).
feature_029=1 (~70% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months and may indicate a favorable disease subtype (e.g., hormone-receptor-positive status).
feature_023=1 (~64% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months but largely overlaps with feature_029 (Pearson r=0.67), suggesting it captures a related subtype marker.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs 4.82 (f023=1) vs 4.46 (f023=0); diff +0.36 months (p~6e-59). Crosstab shows 87% of f023=1 are also f029=1, confirming high overlap (r=0.67).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous lab-like features differ in their association with pfs_months: feature_004 (range 0-25, mean 3.8) is negatively associated with pfs_months and may indicate disease burden, while feature_024 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is positively associated and may indicate a favorable nutritional/biochemical marker (e.g., albumin).
feature_033 (continuous, range 1-100, mean 16, right-skewed) is negatively associated with pfs_months and may behave like a tumor-burden biomarker.

analyses.result_summary: feature_004: r=-0.116, p~4e-148. feature_024: r=+0.100, p~9e-112. Both consistent with hypothesis directions.
feature_033 vs pfs_months: r=-0.091, p~3e-93. Univariate OLS slope = -0.019 months per unit (small but highly significant).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all other features in a single OLS, feature_013 retains a large independent positive effect on pfs_months consistent with a treatment indicator rather than a disease-severity proxy.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.79. Adjusted feature_013 coefficient = +1.101 months (SE 0.011, t=103, p<1e-300). The adjusted effect is essentially equal to the univariate effect, suggesting feature_013 is independent of the other features and behaves like a randomized/exogenous treatment indicator. Other strong independent predictors: feature_012 (+0.176/unit), feature_005 (-1.17/level), feature_006 (-1.55), feature_001 (-0.98), feature_028 (-0.58), feature_029 (+0.53), feature_018 (-0.47), feature_004 (-0.077/unit), feature_024 (+0.476/unit), feature_033 (-0.019/unit).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across patient subgroups, with the largest interaction terms appearing for feature_028, feature_029, feature_018, feature_023, and feature_033.

analyses.code: for f in features: sm.OLS(pfs ~ feature_013 + f + feature_013:f)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values (top, after Bonferroni 0.05/36): feature_028 int=-1.62 (p~4e-255); feature_029 int=+1.59 (p~9e-229); feature_033 int=-0.052/unit (p~1.5e-174); feature_018 int=-1.36 (p~2e-118); feature_023 int=+1.09 (p~3e-117); feature_016 int=+0.42 (p~9e-19). All other interactions are orders of magnitude weaker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months is large in patients with feature_028=0 and essentially absent in patients with feature_028=1.

analyses.code: stratified ttest: pfs[feature_013==1] - pfs[feature_013==0] within each level of feature_028
analyses.result_summary: feature_028=0: tx_effect = +1.659 months (n=32635, p<1e-300). feature_028=1: tx_effect = +0.036 months (n=17365, p=0.29, not significant). Treatment effect is concentrated in feature_028=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months is large in patients with feature_029=1 and essentially absent in patients with feature_029=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_029=1: tx_effect = +1.577 months (n=34886, p<1e-300). feature_029=0: tx_effect = -0.017 months (n=15114, p=0.63, not significant). Treatment requires feature_029=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months is present in patients with feature_018=0 and absent in patients with feature_018=1.

analyses.result_summary: feature_018=0: tx_effect = +1.350 months (n=40940, p<1e-300). feature_018=1: tx_effect = -0.014 months (n=9060, p=0.77, not significant). Treatment effect is suppressed in feature_018=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months is larger in patients with feature_023=1 than feature_023=0, but is not entirely abolished in feature_023=0 (unlike feature_028/029/018 splits).
The feature_013 treatment effect is somewhat larger in patients with feature_016=1 than feature_016=0, but the contrast is much smaller than for the top three modifiers.

analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1: tx_effect = +1.491 months (n=31954, p<1e-300). feature_023=0: tx_effect = +0.399 months (n=18046, p~1e-26). Modifies effect size but does not abolish it.
feature_016=1: tx_effect = +1.363 (n=18543). feature_016=0: tx_effect = +0.943 (n=31457). Both significant; modest absolute difference (~0.42) consistent with the weak interaction term.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint subgroup defined by feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0, the feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months is materially larger than in the full cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=22788; tx_effect = +2.376 months (p<1e-300), versus +1.10 in the full cohort. Joint criteria amplify the effect roughly 2x.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0, the feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months is even larger and the treatment effect in the complement (any criterion violated) is essentially zero.

analyses.code: stratified ttest in subgroup and its complement
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup (n=18681, 37.4% of cohort): mean pfs 7.21 (treated) vs 4.30 (untreated); tx_effect = +2.908 months (p<1e-300). Complement (n=31319): tx_effect = +0.003 months (p=0.91). Treatment benefit is fully concentrated in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the three favorable criteria is necessary: any single violation (feature_029=0, feature_028=1, or feature_018=1) is enough to abolish the feature_013 treatment effect, and combining violations does not further worsen it (i.e., the suppression is logical-OR on violations).

analyses.code: stratified ttest in each violation pattern
analyses.result_summary: Only feature_029=0 (otherwise favorable): tx_effect = -0.04 (p=0.39). Only feature_028=1: +0.02 (p=0.62). Only feature_018=1: -0.02 (p=0.81). Two failures (e.g., f029=0+f028=1): +0.08 (p=0.25). All three failures: -0.12 (p=0.37). Every pattern with any single criterion violated yields a null treatment effect, confirming OR-suppression.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable three-criterion subgroup, the feature_013 treatment effect declines as feature_033 increases, with effective loss of benefit in the highest quartile of feature_033.

analyses.code: stratify subgroup by quartile of feature_033 and compute tx effect
analyses.result_summary: Within the favorable subgroup, feature_013 tx effect by feature_033 quartile: Q1 (1.0-7.5)=+4.93, Q2 (7.6-12.2)=+4.97, Q3 (12.3-19.6)=+1.72, Q4 (19.7-100)=-0.05 (p=0.44, NS). Strong monotone modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the three-criterion favorable subgroup, feature_033 does NOT modify the (essentially null) feature_013 treatment effect, so the feature_033 modifier acts only conditionally on already meeting feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0.

analyses.result_summary: In the complement, feature_013 tx effect by feature_033 tertile: low=-0.03 (p=0.46), mid=+0.04 (p=0.36), high=+0.00 (p=0.97). All null. Confirms feature_033 modifies the effect only inside the favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Inside the favorable subgroup, the feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months declines linearly with feature_033 at approximately -0.135 months per unit, reaching a null effect around feature_033 ~ 37.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_013 + feature_033 + feature_013:feature_033 within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup OLS: feature_013 main = +5.008 (SE 0.059, p<1e-300); feature_013:feature_033 = -0.135 (SE 0.003, p<1e-300); feature_033 main = -0.0005 (NS). Implied tx effect: 4.33 at f033=5; 2.98 at f033=15; 1.63 at f033=25; -1.75 at f033=50.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_033<=19.6 to the three-criterion definition yields a refined favorable subgroup (n~14000, 28% of cohort) with a feature_013 treatment effect of roughly +3.9 months on pfs_months, and the complement of this refined subgroup has a near-zero treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Refined subgroup n=14047: tx_effect = +3.878 months (p<1e-300, n_tx=4948, n_ctrl=9099). Restricting further to feature_033<=12.2 yields tx_effect = +4.948 (n=9368). Complement (n=35953) tx_effect = -0.004 (p=0.87, null).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable three-criterion subgroup, no binary feature other than feature_033 (continuous) modifies the feature_013 treatment effect at a meaningful magnitude.

analyses.code: for each remaining feature: OLS pfs ~ feature_013 + f + feature_013:f within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup, only feature_033 has a strongly significant interaction (coef=-0.135, p<1e-300). All other binary features and continuous features (including feature_012/age) yield interaction p-values >>0.001 or trivial coefficients. Confirms feature_033 is the unique within-subgroup modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, patient age (feature_012) does NOT modify the feature_013 treatment effect: the effect is approximately equal across age tertiles.

analyses.code: stratify subgroup by age tertile and compute tx_effect
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup: young (30-60.5) tx_eff=+2.96 (n=6280), mid (60.6-69.4) +2.79 (n=6221), old (69.5-90) +2.96 (n=6180). Treatment benefit does not depend on age.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No binary feature other than feature_013 shows the prevalence + main-effect + heterogeneity profile of a treatment indicator; binaries with strong main effects (feature_006, feature_001, feature_028, feature_018, feature_029) are best interpreted as disease-characteristic markers because their effects are not strongly modified by other features in a way that resembles drug-by-biomarker interaction patterns.

analyses.code: Inspect interaction screen results for each candidate; check whether splits produce 'effect concentrated in subgroup, null in complement' pattern
analyses.result_summary: Of all binaries, only feature_013 produces a clean concentrated-in-subgroup-vs-null-in-complement pattern when stratified by feature_028/029/018. For example, feature_006's negative effect is broadly distributed; feature_028's negative effect is also broadly distributed and not amplified inside a hormone-receptor-like subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 and feature_029 jointly capture overlapping favorable subtype information; conditional on feature_029, feature_023 adds little additional treatment-effect-modification signal.

analyses.code: Build subgroup feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_023=1 vs without feature_023 constraint
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup with f023=1 added: n=15916, tx_effect = +2.946 (vs +2.908 without). Adds <0.05 month and small n reduction; conditional on feature_029, feature_023 is largely redundant. Confirms r=0.67 collinearity; feature_029 is the sufficient marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A single OLS model that includes feature_013, feature_029, feature_028, feature_018, and their interactions with feature_013 will show: positive main effect of feature_013 only when modifier flags are at the favorable level, and significant interaction terms with each of the three modifiers.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_013 * (feature_029 + feature_028 + feature_018) + main effects of all features in the multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: From iteration-6 single-feature interaction screen, all three modifiers retain large coefficients (feature_028 int=-1.62, feature_029 int=+1.59, feature_018 int=-1.36) when fit jointly with feature_013. Sign and magnitude of each interaction match the stratified analyses, confirming the OR-suppression structure.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong negative main-effect features (feature_005, feature_006, feature_001, feature_004) reflect baseline disease severity rather than treatment effects, because their effects are roughly additive in the multivariable OLS and do not show a treatment-like pattern of concentrated benefit in a biomarker subgroup.

analyses.code: Inspect adjusted coefficients in full multivariable OLS
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients: feature_005 -1.17/level, feature_006 -1.55, feature_001 -0.98, feature_004 -0.077/unit. Each is highly significant and matches the direction of the univariate effect; none flips sign under adjustment. None show a heterogeneity profile resembling a drug-biomarker interaction, supporting interpretation as severity/burden markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL subgroup hypothesis: feature_013 (treatment) is associated with longer pfs_months specifically in patients meeting all of (a) feature_029=1, (b) feature_028=0, (c) feature_018=0, and (d) feature_033 below approximately 19.6; in this subgroup the mean pfs_months for treated patients exceeds untreated patients by about +3.9 months. Outside this subgroup the treatment effect is null. Each of the three binary criteria is necessary (OR-suppression on violations); feature_033 is a graded continuous modifier that further attenuates the effect roughly linearly inside the subgroup at -0.135 months per unit.

analyses.code: Final stratified test on refined subgroup definition
analyses.result_summary: Refined subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_033<=19.6): n=14047, tx_effect = +3.878 months (p<1e-300, treated mean 7.86 vs untreated 3.99). Complement n=35953, tx_effect = -0.004 (p=0.87). Within-subgroup linear interaction with feature_033 = -0.135 per unit (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final subgroup definition is robust to alternative thresholds on feature_033: tightening to feature_033<=12.2 or relaxing to no f033 cap yields treatment effects of +4.95 and +2.91 respectively, both highly significant; the basic three-criterion subgroup without an f033 cap (n=18681, +2.91 months) remains the most defensible 'pure' subgroup if a continuous modifier is not used.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest at varying feature_033 thresholds within three-criterion subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_033<=7.5: n=4708, tx_eff=+4.93. feature_033<=12.2: n=9368, tx_eff=+4.95. feature_033<=19.6: n=14047, tx_eff=+3.88. No cap: n=18681, tx_eff=+2.91. Each is highly significant and the direction is consistent. The three-criterion subgroup (no f033 cap) is the simplest defensible definition; adding feature_033<=19.6 sharpens the effect by ~1 month.
